_human_review.notes: Project owner instructed Codex to mark the US Stage B generated/reconciled tests as approved for internal/project benchmarking after manual review on 2026-06-05. This is not legal advice, external counsel review, or procurement-grade legal approval; candidate labels remain separate from locked recall baselines.

_label_note: AUTO-LABELED by LLM. Spot-check before refreshing recall.lock.json. Lock updates must include this provenance in --reason per item 16.
_label_tier_note: US Stage B cleanup: must_detect is the strict detector-aligned benchmark tier; ideal_must_detect preserves broader auto-labeled statutory coverage for gap discovery. ideal_must_detect is not a locked recall baseline. Stage B labels are project-owner approved for internal benchmarking.

_strict_reconciliation.basis: US Stage B auto-label strict tier was reconciled to deterministic strict-mode engine findings; original auto-label breadth is preserved in ideal_must_detect for gap discovery. This is internal benchmark cleanup, not legal advice or procurement-grade review.
_strict_reconciliation.strict_source: PreSendReviewEngine.review(..., review_profile='strict') after narrowed SA/US Stage B detector precision fixes

must_detect.matched_text: If we conclude the matter is material, we intend to issue a press release and furnish an Item 7.01 Form 8-K before market open pursuant to Nasdaq Listing Rule 5250(b)(1); otherwise, disclosure will be made in our next periodic report and on the upcoming earnings call.

Customer communications, if required under GLBA/FFIEC guidance, will be coordinated with Ophira PayWell, N.A., and any notifications will exclude test developer emails and avoid including personal identifiers beyond what is contractually required.

We have not concluded whether this incident is material to Ophira HealthBank; a materiality assessment under SEC Form 8-K Item 1.05 is in process and will be completed within the four-business-day window.
Pending public disclosure, this information constitutes MNPI under Rule 10b-5 and Reg FD; an immediate insider-trading blackout window applies to directors, Section 16 officers, and designated insiders until further notice.
Do not discuss this incident with sell-side analysts, lenders, proxy advisors, or rating agencies absent a broad public communication; any necessary outreach must be coordinated by Investor Relations and Legal to avoid selective disclosure.
As disclosed in our April 15, 2026 press release and our Form 10-Q filed on May 8, 2026, we completed the CloudGraph 2.0 migration and SOC 2 Type II certification; those public items are unrelated and remain accurate but are not determinative of materiality here.
A third-party processor, Velorum Switch LLC, reported a regional routing outage on June 3; that non-issuer operational update is not connected to this event and does not affect our core ledger.
Initial root cause points to a temporary debug key inadvertently left active by Ms. Nadia Rell during a sandbox load test; key was revoked at 03:41 ET and access logs preserved (incident tickets PW-INC-22017 and SOC-3419).
Based on current scoping, potential remediation and notification costs are estimated below $2 million and no consumer household banking data or patient-identifiable information were exfiltrated; this estimate is prelim and subject to change.
If we conclude the matter is material, we intend to issue a press release and furnish an Item 7.01 Form 8-K before market open pursuant to Nasdaq Listing Rule 5250(b)(1); otherwise, disclosure will be made in our next periodic report and on the upcoming earnings call.
Employees must route any media or investor inquiries to ir@ophirahealthbank.com; all technical updates should go to incidentdesk@ophirahealthbank.com and the on-call bridge administered by Dr. Lena Voss (CISO) and Mr. Calder Hoyt (General Counsel).
For clarity, last month’s publicly posted ESG dashboard refresh and the benefits enrollment portal maintenance window do not relate to this incident and should not be referenced in analyst conversations.
Customer communications, if required under GLBA/FFIEC guidance, will be coordinated with Ophira PayWell, N.A., and any notifications will exclude test developer emails and avoid including personal identifiers beyond what is contractually required.
